title: Document live OpenAI evaluator canary runbook and checkpoint
problem: After the live OpenAI evaluator path is implemented, operators need a concise runbook that distinguishes mock-first internal MVP behavior from opt-in live OpenAI canary behavior, lists pass criteria, explains failure interpretation, and points to the evaluator-to-draft-ticket handoff commands.
acceptance_criteria: README documents the opt-in live OpenAI evaluator canary command, required env gates, cost caps, output path, and pass criteria.
Runtime/config docs explicitly state that .env.local may hold operator-only OpenAI gates and secrets, while shell env overrides file env and public-site code must not read operator env.
Escalation policy docs preserve mock-first defaults and identify live OpenAI synthesis as operator-only candidate output, not accepted graph writes.
Agent report records the latest mock/injected test status, any optional live canary result, no_accepted_graph_writes boundary, and remaining maturity caveats.
Docs do not include API keys, raw prompts, private local source text, or public publish instructions.
test_plan: Optional operator-only live canary: python -m rge.cli synthesize --packet fixtures/synthesis/grounded_evidence_packet_dry_run.json --provider openai --confirm --load-operator-env --output-dir data/tmp/openai_synthesis_canary
non_goals: Do not add new engine features.
Do not make live OpenAI part of CI, golden tests, verify, or execute-safe.
Do not alter public-site runtime behavior or connect it to live graph/API.
Do not commit .env.local or any secret-bearing artifact.
rollback_plan: Revert README, runtime config, escalation policy, and checkpoint report edits only. No DB migration or runtime rollback is required.
